steps.title: Identify both triangles as right isosceles triangles
steps.content:
In triangle PQR with P(4,5), Q(4,7), R(6,5):
Q is directly above P (x = 4) and R is directly right of P (y = 5), so sides PQ and PR are perpendicular. Thus ∠P = 90°.

The distances are |PQ| = 2 and |PR| = 2, so PQ = PR, making PQR an isosceles right triangle.

Similarly, in triangle LMN with L(4,5), M(4,7+k), N(6+k,5):
M is directly above L and N is directly right of L, so ∠L = 90°.

The distances are |LM| = 2+k and |LN| = 2+k, so LM = LN, making LMN an isosceles right triangle.

steps.title: Apply the 45-45-90 triangle property
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle, the right angle is 90° and both other angles must be 45° (since they are equal and sum to 90°).

Therefore:
  • In triangle PQR: ∠Q = 45°, so t = 45°
  • In triangle LMN: ∠N = 45°

steps.title: Express ∠N in terms of t
steps.content:
Since ∠N = 45° and t = 45°, we have:
$$\angle N = 45° = 90° - 45° = 90° - t°$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
